mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2.0% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice equity valuations upward in response to lower cost of capital.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% as of May 2008 indicates near-zero real rates
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades from lower consumer demand and potential recession onset.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% as of May 2008 indicates labor market stress
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1000k units, indicating simultaneous contraction in consumer and residential demand. This joint deterioration signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines 2% MoM AND HOUST falls below 1000k units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1032k units and RSAFS at 385448M as of April 2008 show demand weakness
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month below its 3-month moving average, signaling supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate lower corporate profitability from reduced output and pricing power.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.18 as of April 2008 reflects manufacturing stress
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with falling capacity utilization
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, indicating accelerating labor market deterioration. This joint signal of job losses and rising joblessness produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors price in recession probability and earnings downgrades from lower consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137754k and UNRATE at 5.5% as of May 2008
regime_hint: labor market recession with accelerating job losses
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 3.0% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 1.5%, indicating real interest rates are positive and monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate policy rates will remain elevated, compressing equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.13 and FEDFUNDS at 1.98% as of May 2008
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with low inflation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the real rate interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1000k units annualized, indicating residential construction collapse. This signals demand destruction in housing-related sectors and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice homebuilder and construction-related equity valuations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1032k units as of April 2008 near critical threshold
regime_hint: housing market collapse with construction demand destruction
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 58% of instances when HOUST < 1000k, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) declines quarter-over-quarter while retail sales (RSAFS) fall month-over-month, indicating synchronized contraction in aggregate demand and consumer spending. This dual demand collapse produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors recognize recession onset and reprice corporate earnings expectations sharply downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth < 0.5% AND RSAFS declines 1.5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11701.9B and RSAFS at 385448M as of Q1 2008 show demand weakness
regime_hint: demand-side recession with synchronized GDP and consumer spending contraction
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between GDP and retail sales is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.
